nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal reef environments; grows on hard substrate or free-floating in moderate to high flow zones

summary: Red ornamental macroalgae with deeply furcated, flame-shaped fronds ranging from pink to deep red; generally kept as a display specimen and slow nutrient exporter. Closely related to Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei) and Leafy Ogo (H. maculata).
careNotes: Dragon's Tongue (Halymenia dilatata) is an ornamental red macroalgae prized for its deeply lobed, flame-shaped thallus colored pink to bright red — the origin of its common name. It is closely related to Dragon's Breath (Halymenia durvillei) and Leafy Ogo (Halymenia maculata), all three sharing the same family (Halymeniaceae) and order (Halymeniales). Unlike Dragon's Breath, Dragon's Tongue is native to the Gulf of Mexico, Caribbean, and Tropical Atlantic (including the Red Sea as the type locality), whereas the sibling H. durvillei is Indo-Pacific. AlgaeBarn describes it as tolerating lower light conditions and flourishing in shadier or deeper tank areas, though other sources rate its lighting requirement as medium to medium-high; moderate lighting is generally considered appropriate. Growth rate is described as slow by AlgaeBarn and slow-to-medium in hobby experience, though fishy.wiki rates it fast under optimal conditions — medium is the consensus middle ground. Temperature recommendations vary across sources: AlgaeBarn lists 78–83°F (25.6–28.3°C), fishy.wiki lists 20–27°C, and sibling Halymenia species are typically kept at 22–26°C; an averaged range of approximately 22–27°C is used here. pH 7.8–8.4 and salinity SG 1.023–1.025 are consistent across all sources. Alkalinity 8–12 dKH is recommended. Iron and iodine supplementation may support coloration and growth in some systems. The species can grow free-floating or attached to rock; attached specimens have been reported to exceed 45 cm (1.5 ft) in height. It is generally considered reef-safe but is extremely palatable to tangs, rabbitfish, and many herbivorous fish; placement away from dedicated herbivores is strongly advised. Both nano reef and refugium placement are documented, though its ornamental value makes it most suitable for display. Trident's Cove describes the care level as beginner-friendly, while AlgaeBarn and fishy.wiki rate it as moderate/intermediate; intermediate is used here as the consensus. Propagation in the hobby is primarily by fragmentation (clipping pieces from established colonies). Supplementation with magnesium, iron, and trace elements is beneficial. The AlgaeBase species_id is 1999; the synonym Sebdenia dilatata (Zanardini) De Toni, 1900 is no longer accepted. Note: some trade sources (e.g., K&P Aquaculture) incorrectly apply the "Dragon's Tongue" common name to H. durvillei — this entry follows AlgaeBarn's explicit identification of Dragon's Tongue as H. dilatata.

additional.name: fishy.wiki (Halymenia dilatata)
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters. Agreed on SG 1.023–1.025 and kH 8–12; temperature range 20–27°C vs AlgaeBarn 25.6–28.3°C — averaged to 22–27°C. Disagreed on lighting (medium-high vs low) and growth rate (fast vs slow) — medium used as consensus middle value for both.

additional.name: WoRMS (World Register of Marine Species)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales, class Florideophyceae. Synonym Sebdenia dilatata (Zanardini) De Toni, 1900 confirmed unaccepted.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn blog — Red Macroalgae Refugium Guide
additional.notes: Corroborated low-to-moderate lighting, slow growth rate, ornamental and refugium uses. Size (>1.5 ft attached) consistent with main product page.

additional.name: Reeflings — Halymenia floresii care guide (sibling genus)
additional.notes: Used as sibling-species reference for temperature cross-check only (H. floresii: 22–26°C). pH 8.1–8.4 and SG 1.023–1.025 consistent with primary sources.
